other_observers: Andy Boyce; I was the first to find the bird and ID it
duration: about a minute and a half
specific_location: Two Buttes Reser SWA, below the dam
description: the bird was a large flycatcher, with a body a little smaller than a Western Kingbird, but with very long tail feathers giving the bird a larger look overall.  The head, back, and chest were pale gray. The bird had strikingly bold pink coloring on the underwings and belly. The flight feathers were dark, contrasing with the paler back and head. The bird had very long outer tail feathers, about a third again the length of the body, and much shorter central tail feathers. At times in flight the tail was spread to give the bird a "fork-tailed" look. The tail was mostly black, with much white in the outer tail feathers.  Assumed to be male because of the brightness of the pink coloration and length of the tail.Behaviors: The bird was only seen in flight, first found a couple of hundred yards out as a small speck, but eventually flying right overhead allowing for stunning looks, before dissapearing to the north.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
